godot_contract_refs.section: P2：机制、ID、trace 与 coverage 基础设施

known_ambiguities: Release membership is selected only from validated MechanismSpec target_maturity == RELEASED; computed_status and ImplementationWorkItem completion_status never define the checked set.
The current authoring set contains no mechanisms, so the repository result is a deterministic empty overlay and does not claim a released battle behavior.

A Godot contract edge is bound by ImplementationWorkItem mechanism_ids; project_requirement_keys keeps its existing project-requirement meaning and is not reinterpreted as a work-item ID. A nonempty release-target set requires the contract root, a bounded no-follow whole-document SHA-256 recheck, and an exact ordinal match between every bound section and a column-zero top-level ATX Markdown heading outside fenced code and HTML blocks. Empty authoring remains portable, while historical unbound work-item section labels remain descriptive for compatibility.

An external evidence edge requires every SourceEvidence identity declared by the MechanismSpec to be closed by the P2 source-audit join and exactly matched by battlelogic or pokelib ImplementationWorkItem source references; partial evidence sets, project decisions, and work-item self-reports cannot substitute for full SourceEvidence closure.

A fixture edge requires an actual SCENARIO requirement from the P2 fixture preflight, coverage of the mechanism, and a matching ImplementationWorkItem fixture ID; a work-item-only fixture number does not count.

Noncurrent SourceEvidence remains a present reference with an explicit blocker. Todo 10 generates traceability, coverage, and orphan reports; Todo 11 owns the later hard release failure for stale evidence, unexecuted tests, and other coverage defects.

The P7 production setup compiler remains unavailable. A declared SCENARIO reference can close the static triple, but the overlay records SETUP_COMPILER_UNAVAILABLE_P7 and never claims compilation, execution, pass status, or coverage.

The overlay is read-only and non-runtime. It does not change computed maturity, spec/runtime manifests, fixture payloads, runtime catalogs, reports, timestamps, GUIDs, paths, symbols, source observations, or proprietary content.

Synthetic nonempty tests exercise complete static triples, independent missing legs, five-field evidence identity mismatches, incomplete and noncurrent evidence, mixed work-item review, project-decision substitution, evidence-only project requirements, re-derived audit currentness, cross-artifact consistency checks, output leakage, and the unchanged default maturity gate.

The public validator inspects unmet release targets without publishing its candidate runtime projection, fails when any selected mechanism lacks a static triple, and captures specs, work items, source-audit governance, and fixture declarations from one Repository, Worktree, or Staged view; a nonempty SourceEvidence set still requires the exact ignored sealed P0 audit bytes.
